Name: 5 yearly service hydrant system. 2025. (Quote QD-1700)
Description: 5 yearly service hydrant system. 2025.
Scope of works:
**Subject**: Notification of Upcoming 5-Year Fire Hydrant System Service

We would like to inform you that the scheduled 5-year service for your fire hydrant system is now due. Below, we have outlined the scope of work to be performed:

**On-Site Service Details**:
Site Attendance: A qualified technician will attend your site to carry out the necessary servicing.

**System Isolation & Draining**: The hydrant system will be isolated and fully drained in preparation for maintenance.

**Component Maintenance:** The internal rubber seals will be replaced, and all moving parts will be lubricated to ensure optimal functionality.

**Reassembly & Testing**: After reassembling the system, the valves will be tested to confirm their proper operation.

**Components to be serviced**:

13 x landing valves

1 x booster valve

**Hydrostatic Testing**:
Once all maintenance is complete, we will conduct a hydrostatic test at 1.5 times the system's working pressure. Test results will be recorded for compliance and documentation purposes.

**AS1851 Compliance:**
In line with AS1851 regulations, it is a requirement to replace valve seats and perform a hydrostatic pressure test every 5 years to ensure the continued integrity of the fire hydrant system. This ensures that your system remains compliant with the relevant fire safety standards.

**Scope of Detailed Works**:
Isolate, drain, and reinstate the fire hydrant system.

Strip and service the hydrant landing valves, replacing the internal seat rubbers and lubricating the spindles/shafts.

Perform the hydrostatic pressure test on the system.

Inspect the underground valve and document the findings.

**Hydrostatic Test Procedure**:
The system will be pressurized to 1.5 times the working pressure and held for 60 minutes. Pressure readings will be taken every 10 minutes. Should there be a pressure loss of 10% or more, additional investigation and repairs will be required.

If you have any questions or need further clarification, please don’t hesitate to reach out. We’re here to ensure your fire safety systems are in optimal condition.

Thank you for your attention to this important matter. We look forward to completing the service and maintaining the safety and compliance of your system.

1. Automatic Fire Suppression System
At the time of assessment, the following non-compliances were observed that are required to be rectified:
-  The batteries for both diesel and electric pumps are past the two-year expiry date and need to be replaced.
-  The inlet and outlet tamper valves in the pump room are programmed back to front. The programming needs to be corrected.
-  The warehouse has 5 big ass fans installed under ESFER sprinkler heads. The mech contractor has advised that the fans did not trip on the fire alarm. The fans need to be connected to a fire trip as they will obstruct the flow of water from the sprinkler heads.
-  The fans in the pump room did not start automatically but could be started manually. There appears to be a fire trip that is not connected. The fire trip needs to be connected, and the function tested.

2. Automatic Fire Detection System

Name: 39A287 (Service Quote SQ-0474)
Description: AS1851 5 yearly Major service fire hydrant system.
Scope of works:
**Subject**: Notification of Upcoming 5-Year Fire Hydrant System Service

We would like to inform you that the scheduled 5-year service for your fire hydrant system is now due. Below, we have outlined the scope of work to be performed:

**On-Site Service Details**: Site Attendance: A qualified technician will attend your site to carry out the necessary servicing.

**System Isolation & Draining**: The hydrant system will be isolated and fully drained in preparation for maintenance.

**Component Maintenance:** The whole valve and coupling will be replaced and the old valve and coupling disposed off site.

**Reassembly & Testing**: Test the valves on completion.

40 x landing valves removed and replaced with new valves and couplings.

1 x booster valve overhaul only.

**Hydrostatic Testing**: Once all maintenance is complete, we will conduct a hydrostatic test at 1.5 times the system's working pressure. Test results will be recorded for compliance and documentation purposes.

**AS1851 Compliance:**In line with AS1851 regulations, it is a requirement to replace valve seats and perform a hydrostatic pressure test every 5 years to ensure the continued integrity of the fire hydrant system. This ensures that your system remains compliant with the relevant fire safety standards.

**Scope of Detailed Works**: Isolate, drain, and reinstate the fire hydrant system.

Remove and replace the hydrant landing valve and roll grooved coupling.

Perform the hydrostatic pressure test on the system.

Inspect the underground valve and document the findings.

**Hydrostatic Test Procedure**: The system will be pressurized to 1.5 times the working pressure and held for 60 minutes. Pressure readings will be taken every 10 minutes. Should there be a pressure loss of 10% or more, additional investigation and repairs will be required.

Description: AFSS inspection report December 2025
Scope of works:
Dear Customer,
We hope you're doing well. Following our routine annual testing, we have identified the following defect(s) that we wanted to bring to your attention, please see below:

Section 2 – Assessment results and observations

1.  Automatic Fire Detection & Alarm System
At the time of assessment, the following non-compliances were observed that are required to be rectified:
-  The visual warning devices above the stage did not operate on the fire alarm. The strobes must operate due to the sound levels in the area.
-  There is a heat detector installed under a light fitting that cant be tested, the detector needs to be relocated.
-  There is no detection installed outside the goods lift on ground floor, detection is required in this area.
-  The fire detection block pan does not show the location of the WIP’s on site. The block plan needs to be updated to show this detail.
-  The fire safety certificate that was created for the new fit out lists AS1670.1-2005, this standard does not exist. AS1670.1-2004 Amendment 1 was released in 2005. The block plan lists AS1670.1-2004, further investigation is required.
-  There is a detector installed directly under a ceiling fan in the smoking area that is falling off the roof. Detection can’t be installed closer than 400mm from the blade of the ceiling fan. The detector needs to be relocated.


**Kindly note further quote may be required after initial investigation.** If you have any questions or need further clarification, feel free to reach out.

Thank you for your understanding and continued support.

Name: 5 yearly service hydrant system. 2025. (Quote QD-0518)
Description: 5 yearly service hydrant system. 2025.
Scope of works:
**Subject**: Notification of Upcoming 5-Year Fire Hydrant System Service

We would like to inform you that the scheduled 5-year service for your fire hydrant system is now due. Below, we have outlined the scope of work to be performed:

**On-Site Service Details**:
Site Attendance: A qualified technician will attend your site to carry out the necessary servicing.

**System Isolation & Draining**: The hydrant system will be isolated and fully drained in preparation for maintenance.

**Component Maintenance:** The internal rubber seals will be replaced, and all moving parts will be lubricated to ensure optimal functionality.

**Reassembly & Testing**: After reassembling the system, the valves will be tested to confirm their proper operation.

**Components to be serviced**:

5 x landing valves

1 x booster valve with 4 x booster points.

**Scope of Detailed Works**:
Isolate, drain, and reinstate the fire hydrant system.

Strip and service the hydrant landing valves, replacing the internal seat rubbers and lubricating the spindles/shafts.

Inspect the underground valve and document the findings.

If you have any questions or need further clarification, please don’t hesitate to reach out. We’re here to ensure your fire safety systems are in optimal condition.

Thank you for your attention to this important matter. We look forward to completing the service and maintaining the safety and compliance of your system.

This service normally includes a Hydrostatic test to the system.
We have removed this portion due to the Asbestos in the tower and our Technicians not being allowed in the areas.

Description: AFSS defect inspection report Nov 2025
Scope of works:
Dear Customer,

We hope you're doing well. Following our routine annual testing, we have identified the following defect(s) that we wanted to bring to your attention, please see below:

Section 2 – Assessment results and observations

1.  **Failsafe Devices (these maybe required to be completed by the security company after initial inspection)**
-  Although the schedule is missing references to BCA D2.19, the front automatic doors should be linked to the fire alarm system and open on a fire alarm. This gives fire brigade access to the fire panel without the need to smash the front doors.
-  L10, L7, L3, L6, L2 only have access to one set of fire stairs due to card readers or doors that lock from the side of egress, if fire obstructs the accessible fire stairs from the lift, access to a second exit is required. The doors need to be modified with either emergency break glass door releases, a fire trips that release the doors on a fire alarm or change the lockset to allow access from the side of egress without the use of a key.

4.  EWIS
At the time of assessment, the following non-compliances were observed that are required to be rectified:
-  Level 2 has no occupant warning speakers installed, no tones or PA could be heard. A complete review of this floor is required (technicians to confirm DB levels, there are no speakers in the rear of the tenancy or any rooms).

6.  Automatic Fire Detection System
At the time of assessment, the following non-compliances were observed that are required to be rectified:
-  L10, the office named go away is missing the smoke detector.
-  L9, there is an office with no smoke detector installed, 1 x detector required.
-  L8, there are caps on all detectors on the floor that need to be removed, and one detector is missing from outside the lift.
-  L5, there is no detection in both toilets, 2 x detectors required.
-  L5, there is no detector in the office of suit 2, 1 x detector required.
-  L5, there is no detection installed in five offices of suit 3. Access was not provided to this area last year.

Thank you for your understanding and continued support.

Name: 39A460 (Service Quote SQ-0458)
Description: 5 yearly major servicing AS1851 Fire hydrant system.
Scope of works:
**Subject**: Notification of Upcoming 5-Year Fire Hydrant System Service

We would like to inform you that the scheduled 5-year service for your fire hydrant system is now due. Below, we have outlined the scope of work to be performed:

**On-Site Service Details**:
Site Attendance: A qualified technician will attend your site to carry out the necessary servicing.

**System Isolation & Draining**: The hydrant system will be isolated and fully drained in preparation for maintenance.

**Component Maintenance:** The internal rubber seals will be replaced, and all moving parts will be lubricated to ensure optimal functionality.

**Reassembly & Testing**: After reassembling the system, the valves will be tested to confirm their proper operation.

**Components to be serviced**:

25 x landing valves

1 x booster valve

**Hydrostatic Testing**:
Once all maintenance is complete, we will conduct a hydrostatic test at 1.5 times the system's working pressure. Test results will be recorded for compliance and documentation purposes.

**AS1851 Compliance:**In line with AS1851 regulations, it is a requirement to replace valve seats and perform a hydrostatic pressure test every 5 years to ensure the continued integrity of the fire hydrant system. This ensures that your system remains compliant with the relevant fire safety standards.

**Scope of Detailed Works**:
Isolate, drain, and reinstate the fire hydrant system.

Strip and service the hydrant landing valves, replacing the internal seat rubbers and lubricating the spindles/shafts.

Perform the hydrostatic pressure test on the system.

Inspect the underground valve and document the findings.

**Hydrostatic Test Procedure**:
The system will be pressurized to 1.5 times the working pressure and held for 60 minutes. Pressure readings will be taken every 10 minutes. Should there be a pressure loss of 10% or more, additional investigation and repairs will be required.

If you have any questions or need further clarification, please don’t hesitate to reach out. We’re here to ensure your fire safety systems are in optimal condition.

Thank you for your attention to this important matter. We look forward to completing the service and maintaining the safety and compliance of your system.

**Booster flow testing**

**Complete booster flow testing with pump truck.**

Name: Blackwoods – 1-3 Bellevue Circuit, Greystanes tank 10yr servicing (Quote QD-0074)
Description: Blackwoods – 1-3 Bellevue Circuit, Greystanes tank 10yr servicing
Scope of works:
The following quotation is based on requirements to produce a 10yr condition report for the fire Hydrant system water supply tank.

Scope of works: Service and Condition Report for the amount of: One (1) @ 366kL Fire Sprinkler Water Storage Tank
 
1.	Any site specific inductions (FlameSafe & contractors).
2.	Complete all relevant site documentation (confined space entry contractor)
3.	Make required system & pump isolations.
4.	Drain tank water (utilizing tank drain point to nearest storm water drain pit)
5.	Remove side entry manway.
6.	Place gas monitor within the tank and complete confined space paperwork.
7.	Set up confined space safety equipment.
8.	Set up pump and remove the residual water from the base of the tank (we have only allowed to pump the last 50mm of water)
9.	Removal of sludge and debris from tank (pumped to waste).
10.	Set up internal scaffold if required.
11.	Clean internal walls and floor surfaces of tank.
12.	Clean internal tank components.
13.	Full visual tank inspection and photographic report of the internal condition of the tank.
14.	Full visual tank inspection and photographic report of the external condition of the tank.
15.	Service Certificate provided upon completion of works to Client.


Twelve Thousand Eight Hundred & Forty Four Dollars: $6,440.00 – excludes GST

BASIS OF QUOTATION

-	The above quotation has been supplied based on the information supplied and extent of works detailed, however Flamesafe Fire Protection reserve the right to change, vary or modify the installation based on site conditions, without any adjustment to the contract value.

-	The above quotation, is based on all materials and equipment being stored on site during the execution of the above works. The works are estimated to be completed over a 4day period, pending any issues noted once the tanks are drained.

-	Confined Space: Should the tank construction area be defined as a confined space, rather than a restricted access area, any additional person will be required on site to act as a spotter. The client shall provide Tasman Tanks a suitably qualified person to perform the task at hand. The additional labour shall be charged in accordance with the standard site rates if supplied by Tasman Tanks. Additional personnel shall be arranged prior to tank construction.

EXCLUSIONS

-	Works to the base building area of site.
-	Work outside normal working hours.
-	Whilst the report indicates rusted internal parts, this is not conclusive till tank is drained and cleaned, Our quotation specifically excludes for the replacement of the tank liner or attached internal pipe work & or fittings if required unless specifically stated.
-	Any detrimental effect on the sealing components within the tank
-	Provision for existing defects not identified at the time of inspection.
-	Cutting and rectification of access panels to allow access to existing pipe work, where & if required.
-	Building works, associated with the above.
-	No allowance for any cost to store, collect or remove rubbish on site. Rubbish removal is done using bins provided by the client on site. Tasman will place rubbish into the bins provided by the client.
-	All asbestos related works are excluded
-	Any works not detailed above.

Clarifications:

-	There shall be a safe and reasonable way to access the tank, we have not included in our price and costs for access such as scaffolding and EWP’s etc. Delays due to access will incur additional charges.
-	There shall be access to power within a reasonable distance to the tank.
-	There shall be adequate lighting outside the tank in order to perform the quoted works.
-	There shall be access to water within 20m of the tank.
-	The pricing covers only the work covered in the Scope of works above, any additional work required outside the scope of work will be charged additionally.
